title: oenothera deltoides subsp. howellii
intro: Oenothera deltoides subsp. howellii, also known as Howell's evening primrose, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, Howell's evening primrose can be found in salves, creams, and tinctures, often used topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
